title: sideroxylon baillonii
intro: Sideroxylon baillonii, also known as the Liqueur Tree or Bael tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapotaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The plant's bark, leaves, and fruit have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, such as fever, diarrhea, and skin infections. Herbal preparations of Sideroxylon baillonii include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to promote digestive health, reduce inflammation, and boost the immune system.
